landing.subtitle: Savings groups with real commitment. A single 5% protocol fee on penalties keeps SafeNudge running — the rest goes to members who showed up.
landing.trustLine: Vault protected by an audited contract. No admin key, no custody.
landing.previewNudgeBody: Ana finished her deposit. Your turn by Friday.
landing.momentsTitle: Deposit, get a heads-up, hit the goal.
landing.moment1Body: One tap. Your wallet signs. The vault records.
landing.moment2Body: If someone's late, the group gets a gentle reminder — no one has to argue.
landing.moment3Body: End of cycle, the contract releases to those who showed up. Automatic.
landing.whyTitleTail: It's the oldest way of saving there is.
landing.whyIntro: Consórcio, caixinha, vaquinha, junta, tanda. Academics call it ROSCA — a group that agrees on an amount, a schedule, and trusts each other to close the cycle. SafeNudge is the same idea — with the tedious parts (chasing, reminding, holding) solved in a contract.
landing.stat1Label: more savings with a commitment device
landing.stat2Label: higher balance in peer-savings accounts vs. solo accounts
landing.stat2Cite: Kast, Meier & Pomeranz · Review of Econ & Stat · 2018
landing.stat3Label: Brazilians in active consórcios — the country's oldest rotating-group savings model
landing.stat4Label: more than control: fixed-installment commitment saved $10.20 vs $0.64 in 130 days
landing.stat4Cite: John · When Commitment Fails · Field experiment · 2018
landing.stat5Label: more effective having an external referee than promising yourself
landing.stat5Cite: Ariely · Harvard · self-imposed vs. external deadlines
landing.stat6Label: success rate when money is on the line and someone verifies — the group is that referee
landing.quoteText: You can't save alone. People join a group
landing.quoteCite: — ROSCA participants quoted in Gugerty · U. Washington · 2007
landing.quoteExplainer: What makes it work is what the literature calls a commitment device: the deposit is public, and you can't pull it back. Showing up or not stops being a private decision — it becomes a conversation with people you respect.

transaction.successDetail: Your transaction was sent and confirmed successfully on the network
transaction.securityNote: Never share your recovery phrase. SafeNudge never asks for your password outside the wallet.

comoFunciona.sub: Every SafeNudge group moves through the same three tempos: first you agree, then you deliver together, then you receive. The vault only releases money when each part happens at the rhythm you chose.

pillars.name: Agree
pillars.lede: Before the first cent, the group decides everything — together, in plain words. After that, the contract remembers.

points.title: Who's in
points.body: Only people you invite. Groups are private — no one discovers them, no one asks to join. Max 20, min 3.

points.title: How much, when
points.body: Monthly amount, deposit day, cycle length. The group picks — R$100 or R$5,000, 3 months or 24.

points.title: Late penalty rate
points.body: The group sets a percentage (e.g. 5%) deducted from anyone who's late. That deduction becomes a bonus for members who followed through — and that's the only place SafeNudge earns (small fraction).

points.title: It all becomes contract
points.body: The rules become Anchor code on Solana. No one — not us, not the creator — can change them later without a vote.

pillars.name: Deliver
pillars.lede: Month by month, each person deposits. The money sits in the vault until cycle end. When someone forgets, the group reminds — no drama, no collection calls.

points.title: Nudge before
points.body: The app nudges 3 days before, 1 day before, morning of. Gentle. No red letters, no scare tactics.

points.title: Group heads-up
points.body: If the day passes, any member can send a heads-up. A notification arrives — no amount, no demand, just a human reminder.

points.title: Automatic penalty
points.body: 48h after the delay, the contract deducts the percentage the group agreed to. That amount goes into the bonus for members who followed through — and SafeNudge keeps a small fraction.

points.title: All visible
points.body: Who's deposited, who's missing, how much is saved. No ranking, no shame — just transparency so no one gets lost.

pillars.name: Receive
pillars.lede: In the final month, the vault releases to everyone at once. No order, no draw, no "who goes first" — you started together, you finish together.

points.title: Everyone in the same moment
points.body: It's not a rotating caixinha. Each person receives the total they deposited plus their share of the group's accumulated penalty bonus.

points.title: Instant
points.body: The transaction happens in seconds on Solana. No business days, no clearing, no manual release — just the contract executing.

points.title: Almost everything goes back to people who showed up
points.body: You get your full deposit back. The only deductions are Solana network fees (fractions of a cent) and the 5% protocol fee on the penalty pool — the other 95% of penalties becomes a bonus for compliant members.

points.title: And it cycles again
points.body: At cycle end, the group decides: run again with the same crew, change the amount, or wrap. It's a caixinha, not a prison.

comoFunciona.defaultsSub: We designed the worst case first. Here's what the contract does — automatically — when a deposit doesn't land.

defaults.body: Only the missing person sees it. No one else is notified. Most late deposits are just forgetfulness.
Any member can send a heads-up. Gentle notification, no amount, no demand.
Agreed percentage (e.g. 5%) is deducted from the next deposit. 95% becomes a bonus for members who followed through; the remaining 5% is the protocol fee.
The group votes on replacing. Penalty bonus already deducted accrues to those who stayed.

comoFunciona.defaultsFoot: In 94% of cycles, only Day 0 happens. The rest exists for when you need it.

faq.q: Do I have to trust everyone in the group?
faq.a: You need to trust the vault. The escrow and automatic rules cover the moments when human trust fails — because it does sometimes.

faq.q: Who holds the money while it waits?
faq.a: The contract. Not SafeNudge, not the group creator, not a partner bank. The balance is visible on-chain the whole time.

Not on your deposit. SafeNudge takes 5% of the penalty pool — only on what gets deducted from latecomers. The other 95% becomes a bonus for members who followed through. If nobody is late in a cycle, we don't charge anything. You still pay Solana network fees, which are fractions of a cent per transaction.

You can, but your deposits already in the vault stay there until cycle end — everyone receives together at the end. If you leave without completing, the agreed penalty kicks in and becomes a bonus for the rest of the group.

faq.q: What if SafeNudge goes out of business?
faq.a: The vault keeps working. It's an open contract on the blockchain — you can recover everything directly, even without the app. We make things easier, but we don't lock you in.

faq.q: Does the money earn yield while it waits?
faq.a: If the group wants, yes — there's a stablecoin yield option with treasury-rate returns. Opt-in, with a visible button to turn it off.

comoFunciona.ctaSub: Create a group with people who already trust you. Or join one with an open seat.

cancelGroup.intro: As the creator, you can end the group at any time. Each member gets back exactly what they deposited. No one is penalized — including you.
cancelGroup.refundBody: Each member receives their full deposit, in the same USDC, to the same wallet they joined with.
cancelGroup.noPenaltyBody: Accumulated penalties are cancelled. The vault is zeroed and closed.
cancelGroup.autoBody: Once you confirm, the contract releases the funds. It never passes through us.
cancelGroup.thinkAgainBody: If a member is struggling, it's worth talking to the group before cancelling everything.
cancelGroup.confirmBody: This will end {{groupName}} and return {{amount}} to {{count}} wallets. There's no undoing.
cancelGroup.signNote: You'll sign a Solana transaction. SafeNudge never touches the money — the refund is made directly by the contract.

seguranca.h1l2: It's a list of things we made impossible.
seguranca.sub: In 2026, a Solana protocol lost $285M in 12 minutes. It wasn't a bug — it was a human with an admin key and the wrong call. SafeNudge was designed so that kind of mistake simply can't exist.
seguranca.pillarsTitle: Four things no one — not even us — can do
seguranca.pillarsSub: Each group's vault is an audited contract with no back door. These are the guarantees you get by default, without asking.

pillars.title: No admin key
pillars.body: There's no button we can press to move money out of your vault. The contract was published with no upgrade privilege — not us, not anyone, can change the rules later.

pillars.title: No custody
pillars.body: Your money never passes through SafeNudge's account. It goes straight from your wallet to the group vault, and from the vault to the receiver's wallet. Nothing parked with us, ever.

pillars.title: Rules locked at start
pillars.body: When the cycle begins, rules turn to stone. Amount, frequency, penalty, order — none of it changes mid-cycle. Changes only by group consensus, in a new cycle.

pillars.title: Everything public on-chain
pillars.body: Every deposit, every payout, every penalty — recorded in auditable blocks. You can verify the vault balance without our app, anytime.

seguranca.openSub: We don't have a third-party audit — yet. What we do have is all the code public, in readable Rust, on GitHub. Before you trust us with a savings group, read it (or ask someone to).

openPoints.body: Full Anchor contract + React frontend + integration tests. No 'secret part', no server that holds your key. If you read Rust, you can read everything.
Before mainnet deploy, we'll contract an independent audit (Trail of Bits, ClawSecure or OtterSec). For now, we're only on devnet — no real money on the line.
When we go to mainnet, we'll open a bounty program for anyone who finds a critical vulnerability. The vault is worth more open than closed.

drift.kicker: The Drift case, in one line
drift.title: The attack that shaped our design.
drift.body: In April 2026, Drift Protocol lost $285M in 12 minutes. It wasn't a code bug — it was social engineering, compromised governance, and a manipulated oracle. The lesson: security isn't just correct code. It's correct code + correct constraints + correct operational assumptions.
checks: No human governance over funds — only code
No external oracle (we don't need one)
No key that can be compromised by phishing or durable nonces
One vault per group, not a central pool — limited blast radius

seguranca.moneySub: You're not held hostage. The vault keeps existing on-chain, with the same rules, without needing our app.

moneyPoints.title: The vault is yours, not ours
moneyPoints.body: Each group has its own address on Solana. Our app is just a pretty interface for it.

moneyPoints.title: There's an emergency app
moneyPoints.body: We maintain a minimal open-source client that runs in your browser, no server. Lets you recover funds from any vault, even if the official site disappears.

moneyPoints.title: Automatic payout at cycle end
moneyPoints.body: Even if no one presses anything, the contract releases funds to all members at cycle end. There's no manual lock.

faq.q: Can you divert money from my group?
faq.a: No. The contract was published with no instruction that moves funds to an arbitrary address. Not with our key, not with the group creator's. Only to validated members, at cycle end.

faq.q: What if a group member is malicious?
faq.a: They can be late or vanish. They can't touch anyone else's vault. The penalty system guarantees that those who follow through never take the loss — they get a bonus instead.

faq.q: How do you make money without charging members?
faq.a: When someone is late, a small fraction of the penalty the group set goes to SafeNudge. The rest becomes a bonus for those who delivered. If the whole group completes the cycle, we earn zero — and that's fine, it's the signal that it worked.

faq.q: Why Solana and not another blockchain?
faq.a: Network fees in fractions of a cent (matters for small deposits), confirmation in seconds (not minutes), and native USDC. Alternatives were too expensive or too slow for a R$100 monthly caixinha.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: Minimum possible. Group name, optional photo, email if you want reminders. No CPF, no ID, no proof of address. The blockchain proves you participated.

faq.q: What if I lose my wallet key?
faq.a: We recommend wallets with social recovery (Phantom, Backpack). If you still lose access, any group member can vote to replace you and recover your proportional balance.

seguranca.ctaSub: Create a group with people who already trust you. The rest of the work — remembering, collecting, distributing — stays with the vault.
